title: Oil Surges After Trump Says US Ceasefire With Iran Is Over
summary: Oil jumped after US President Donald Trump said a tentative ceasefire with Iran was over, raising the prospect of a fresh round of conflict in one of the world’s most important energy-producing regions.

title: Pricey Credit Markets Fuel Funds That Can Buy Anything
summary: Bond investors are increasingly betting on flexible funds that can buy whatever they like as lofty credit market valuations leave little margin for error.

title: Dream Finders Homes Boosts Bid for Beazer to $875 Million
summary: Dream Finders Homes Inc. boosted its takeover offer for rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc., which said it’s received interest from other potential suitors.

title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Truce Comes Under Strain: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell, bond yields rose and oil rallied after President Donald Trump thrust geopolitical risks back into focus by saying the ceasefire between the US and Iran may be over.

title: US Stock Futures Slide After Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is Over
summary: US equity futures fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

title: Omani Shipping Firm Asyad’s Owner Is Said to Weigh Stake Sale
summary: A conglomerate backed by Oman’s sovereign wealth fund is looking to sell a stake worth in the shipping business it listed last year, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Ather Is Said to Plan $200 Million India Fundraise
summary: Ather Energy Ltd. is planning a share sale to institutional investors as early as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, to raise as much as $157 million.

title: European Bonds Slide as Oil Spike Reignites Inflation Fears
summary: European bonds slumped, sending French borrowing costs to their highest since 2009, as oil prices and inflation expectations rose after President Donald Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran.

title: Stocks Drop, Oil Jumps After Trump Says Ceasefire with Iran Is "Over" | Bloomberg Brief 07/08/2026
summary: US equity futures slump and oil extends gains as President Trump says a tentative ceasefire with Iran is over. The US had struck 80 sites in Iran ahead of the president's remarks at the NATO Summit in Ankara, Turkey. Mark McCormick of BMO Capital Markets discusses the impact of fresh geopolitical tensions.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oman Fertilizer Firm Defies War Worries and Surges 23% on Debut
summary: Oman India Fertiliser Co. jumped more than 23% on its Muscat trading debut after raising 261 million Omani rials ($678 million) in the region’s largest initial public offering of the year.

title: Romania Keeps EU’s Highest Rates With Inflation Stuck Above 10%
summary: Romania held interest rates at the highest level in the European Union as policymakers tackle double digit inflation in an economy on the verge of a recession.

title: Susquehanna Puts Up $500 Million for ‘Hedging’ World Cup Games
summary: Susquehanna International Group has lined up $500 million to work with institutions that want to use prediction markets to hedge the economic risks associated with various World Cup outcomes.

title: AI Firm Zhipu to Sell $4 Billion of Shares After 1,500% Rally
summary: Chinese artificial-intelligence model maker Zhipu is seeking to raise about $4 billion from a share sale after its stock soared almost 1,500% since a January listing in Hong Kong.

title: Indian Stocks, Rupee Slide After Trump Reignites Iran Tensions
summary: Indian stocks tumbled and the rupee weakened after oil prices gained following US President Donald Trump’s remark that the tentative ceasefire with Iran had ended after recent attacks on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Coffee Prices to Stay High as Volatility Lingers, Lavazza Says
summary: The latest bout of volatility in the coffee market is set to linger, complicating the outlook for consumers hoping to see lower prices, according to Italian roaster Luigi Lavazza SpA.

title: Argentina to Repay $4 Billion, Defying Critics Who Doubted Tack
summary: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible while the country refuses to tap global debt markets.

title: Trump & Iran Back in Focus, Korean Stocks Tumble | The Opening Trade 7/8/2026
summary:
Stocks fell, bond yields spiked and oil rallied after President Donald Trump thrust geopolitical risks back into focus by declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran to be over.

The AI rotation trade is gathering pace in Asia as investors pull money from the chipmakers that powered this year’s rally and hunt for cheaper ways to play the technology boom. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Guy Johnson. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Heat Sends Power Demand Far Beyond Forecasts
summary: The country’s biggest grid operator says consumption likely hit a record, smashing a high that had stood for about 20 years.

title: State Bank of India Draws Over $1.5 Billion in Offshore Deposits
summary: State Bank of India has so far raised more than $1.5 billion from foreign-currency deposits under a special program launched last month for overseas citizens, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Allianz Unit to Cut as Many as 1,800 Jobs in Push to Adopt AI
summary: Allianz SE plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as the company pushes forward with implementing artificial intelligence solutions.

title: Soybean Oil Hits Three-Week High as US-Iran Ceasefire Dissolves
summary: Chicago soybean oil futures climbed to a three-week high after fresh US military strikes on Iran sparked a rally in crude prices, raising the prospect of greater demand for biofuel feedstocks.

title: Wave of Earnings Surprises Will Be Hard to Repeat, Says Goldman
summary: The wave of AI-fueled earnings surprises that powered stocks in the last earning season will be tough to repeat, making it unlikely the results alone will spark a major rally, according to Christian Mueller-Glissmann.

title: India Seeks Safe Hormuz Passage for Nine Ships as Truce Falters
summary: India is considering options including holding talks with Iran in order to secure safe passage for at least nine laden tankers waiting in the Persian Gulf, as attacks in the waterway threaten an already fragile interim ceasefire.

title: Trump Says US-Iran Ceasefire Is ‘Over’ After Strikes | The Pulse 7/8/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Mike Bell, RBC BlueBay Market Strategy Head; Ionela Ciolan, Wilfried Martens Centre for European Studies Research Officer  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nvidia Is World’s Most Valuable Company and Cheaper Than Hershey
summary: Nvidia is relatively cheap despite the continued domination of its chips in AI data centers.

title: Gold Drops on Rate-Hike Fears as Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over
summary: Gold dropped as US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran has ended, stoking concerns that a renewal of fighting could again drive inflation and push up interest rates.

title: Tesco Explores Sale of Eastern European Business, FT Reports
summary: Tesco Plc is exploring a sale of its central and eastern European operations, the FT reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

title: Bangladesh Explores Debut Sovereign Bond Sale Overseas
summary: Bangladesh is studying a plan to issue its first-ever sovereign bond overseas, as the nation looks for ways to finance its record spending.

title: India Clears New Derivatives on Index Designed for Foreigners
summary: India’s market regulator has approved the launch of derivatives on an index designed specifically for foreign investors.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Extend Loss as Oil Jumps on Iran Risks
summary: Emerging-market stocks extended losses as an escalation in the Middle East conflict added to the ongoing rotation out of AI beneficiaries.
